tldr: Replace Braze with the open-source Apprise notification library — only viable for transactional/notification use cases, not full lifecycle marketing.
why_switch: Eliminate per-MAU SaaS costs in favor of OSS
Simple, scriptable notification API across many backends
Good fit for ops/dev notifications and basic transactional sends
what_to_export: List of users and their preferred notification channels
Device tokens or webhook endpoints relevant to notifications
Email and SMS contact info (if you'll route via Apprise backends)
Triggering events from your backend that should fire notifications
Segment criteria you intend to recreate in your own code
export_method: Use Braze's user and subscription export capabilities per their official documentation to pull contact info, device data, and consent state. Capture transactional template content from the dashboard. Note that journey/Canvas logic will not translate — only the notification trigger points are relevant.
import_method: Install Apprise (Python library / CLI) per its official README and configure the notification backends you need (e.g. APNs, FCM, email, SMS providers, webhooks) using Apprise's URL-based configuration. Move trigger logic into your own backend services so they call Apprise when events occur. You will own segmentation, scheduling, A/B testing, and analytics.

concept_mapping.note: Apprise doesn't orchestrate journeys — your code does
Define and run segments in your own data layer
Configure per Apprise's documented URL syntax
Render strings yourself before passing to Apprise
Manage opt-in state in your own DB
Apprise doesn't track opens, clicks, or deliverability

gotchas: Apprise is a notification library, not a marketing platform — segmentation, journeys, A/B testing, and analytics are your responsibility
No built-in deliverability dashboards or opt-out flows
Push at scale requires you to operate APNs/FCM credentials and handle token lifecycle
Compliance (GDPR/CAN-SPAM) becomes the team's burden — no vendor guardrails
Loss of Liquid/templating sophistication — rewrite into your stack
Best suited to transactional/devops notifications, not marketing campaigns; reconsider if you need lifecycle messaging

email.subject: Evaluating Braze → Apprise (OSS) migration
email.body:
Hi team,

We're scoping a move from Braze to the open-source Apprise notification library to eliminate SaaS costs and own our notification stack.

Important caveat: Apprise is a notification library, not a marketing platform. It handles delivery across many backends, but segmentation, journeys, templating, A/B testing, and analytics would move into our own services.

Proposed scope:
1) Identify which Braze use cases are truly transactional vs lifecycle marketing.
2) For transactional flows, integrate Apprise and move trigger logic into our backend.
3) Decide separately whether lifecycle marketing stays on a SaaS or is rebuilt internally.
4) Migrate consent/contact data and re-implement opt-out flows ourselves.

Estimated effort: 2-12 weeks depending on how much we choose to absorb. I'll share a detailed scoping doc.

Thanks
